item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Props to the image/licensing team on this: I didn't realize that there were new images on here until I looked at the history + image citations. Good on ya.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i do NOT want to see that face EVER again thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After years and years, it just occurred to me out of the blue at work that the reason they go from sending two big male convicts to a relatively unimposing young woman is they wanted someone that they thought the monster could believe was weak enough risk attacking.

item.title: Re: IMAGES CAPTAIN POST
item.content:encoded: rip in the chat for the old images

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the new images makes me feel queasy but not in a "ugh i hate it" way, but more in a "this looks wrong" way. i like it a lot, fits the theme.

item.title: IMAGES CAPTAIN POST
item.content:encoded:
The images that were used in this article have been replaced with the following images, which are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: SCP Foundation Wiki
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: "087face.jpg"
Author: CityToast
Release year: 2020

Title: "087stair.png"
Author(s): Josh Plueger, Aeou, CityToast
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by CityToast utilizing the images below.

Source: Offutt Air Force Base
License: Public Domain
Title: Dorm Cleaning
Author: Josh Plueger

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 4.0
Title: 6 Dontsova Street, Lviv (06).jpg
Author: Aeou

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just endless stairs. is that a face

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna lie, when I first heard about this, I honestly wasn't that scared. I was pretty much just thinking about some stairs with a spooky face dude and a child crying near the bottom. When I read the exploration logs, I actually got decently scared. Pretty good article and a decent story, especially for a Series 1. Not the best article of all time, but still not too bad. With some revision, this might be able to be sufficient for Series VI! The idea certainly is!

Anyhow, I made this pilgrimage to give a +1, and that's what I'll do.

item.title: Exploration IV Never Existed
item.content:encoded: Close inspection of SCP-087's page history shows that Exploration IV never existed. Zaeyde only added the links in edit 1, and he put [DATA EXPUNGED] on Exploration 4 and never made a link.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: I think this is one of my Favorite SCPs, like, yeah its scary as all hell but just how elaborate it is and relate able and seemingly [more] realistic just makes it all the more enticing. i really like that cliff hanger. the author did a wonderful job of giving us just enough info to try to predict what is in that last exploration log but nothing concrete. i hope there are more by the same person (and if thats not possible then cool, ill try to find tales or something idk) super cool! hate teh face tho, in a good way! its just terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Allow me to introduce you to my good friend Mokey, who experienced that firsthand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… is this thing floating or is its face the only thing illuminated? God knows what it’s body looks like if it has one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I wanna know is what would happen if you fell over the rail and kept falling…

item.title: 一个好主意
item.content:encoded: 能不能把这玩意变成一个无限的垃圾池？

item.title: some new thought
item.content:encoded: may be they found the crying girlscp 087-2 in the last time,they found it was not a human but more terrorfying,then they all dead ,so the foundation forbid anyone coming in scp087 again…..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks fine to me. The image is hosted offsite, so it's possible that your browser is blocking the website it's hosted on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something is wrong with the Figure A image can the admins fix it please? I can't since the page is locked for editing. It really takes away a lot of the creepiness with the image missing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pants? Pooped.
Fear of dark stairs? Revived.
Paranoia? Taken up to eleven.

Yep, this is one of those articles

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry if this counts as a necropost, but:

  • This is a nice SCP
  • The face image was pasted into the stair image. (In the attached image, you can see a rectangular space lacking the surrounding noise around the face. It doesn’t really matter and isn’t losing an upvote, though [[https://ibb.co/SBjgWx0]]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: by far my favourite scp. i hope something else like this gets created soon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey :) I just wanted to comment on this specific old scp, it was what made me a fan and kept me interested in the site as well as 97, I love the logs, and the idea. Other then the typo others have pointed out, it really is special.

the pacing is quite well done, especially considering it is just walking down a flight of stairs, and the D class seem quite believable. Im glad this gets the recognition it deserves, and I hope one day to submit something as good (or even close, haha)

I would love to see more in this, like several ideas brought up in these discussions, like "why not send a drone down" or "lower a camera on a string/cable down the middle" or have the ending expanded on like "meeting the thing that seems to be a young girl" or a newer exploration log where the girl is the same age.

regardless, ty for this work, it is art to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate the fact the the writer skiped the reveal, but at the same time I understant that it makes the SCP so much better.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: CorrectHorse, the above comment technically contributes a meaningful discussion point, and is not spam. As such, it's fine. Please let staff handle addressing necroposts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please do not necropost

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because generally for high level explorations D's most likely need a decent level of education and such know how to measure distance correctly?

The first guy was a researcher before so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How come all of the Ds correctly guessed that the voice is exactly about 200 meters down?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably my favorite horror SCP out here, because it makes us fear things other than death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who knows? They never say what it's made out of, it is just a face, but saying that would say that hundreds of other SCPs are immortal. I would like to think that it's SCP-087 monitoring anyone who is exploring it. But there is no real evidence for this. The most likely explanation is that it's a whole other entity and that it is just…watching whoever is down that far in 087. I am probably wrong, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for me only one scp scared me and thats mal.io

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: i don't get that seeing vital info redacted inst spooky its just unsatisfying

item.title: Re: Audio?
item.content:encoded: TheVolgun did a reading of this one on YouTube complete with acted out versions of the first two explorations. It's pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but this is horror, and we all know that its very rare that people in horror novels, stories, or movies use common sense.

Actually, from an in-universe sort of sense, perhaps they've tried that already, and it was severed at some point, etc. or they didn't have enough rope to go down enough.

Besides; it's presumed to be an infinite amount of staircases. So…..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would need to be a long line but I see your point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realize this is a weird nitpick to have, but…why has no one just dropped a camera on a line down? no reason it wouldn't fit. The picture shows a gap between the railings. Seems easier and safer (in several senses).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this a while ago and just now was looking trough top rated pages of all time. As soon as i clicked on 087 and saw the staring face, I got goosebumps. Great job.

item.title: Audio?
item.content:encoded: This is awesome, but maybe it'd be better with audio? I think it'd be cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this is old it's already at 2076! Hope you got lucky!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is SCP-087-1 immortal?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Thanks for bringing this to staff attention! Since it seems fairly obvious that there is a missing word, I've added it in. I don't think there would be words that are a better fit than "presumed", but if anyone has alternate suggestions of more clinical or tone-consistent wording, by all means respond with them here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a typo in the second paragraph of the description: "…from what is to be a child …"
A word is missing there, presumably "…presumed to be…"

This is just slightly too big an edit on too high-profile an article for me to fix unilaterally.
I also see that this has been pointed out by other commenters. But since that was at least two pages of comments back and no fix yet; I'm bringing it up again to call attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know those stories where you don't want to read it, but it continues calling your name?

087 is calling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well-executed, FUCKING CREEPY, GODDAMIT, and those Exploration Logs… GAAAAH!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. The transcripts of the expeditions entertained me almost as much as reading the actual article. +1 from me for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a timeless masterpiece. Great horror, great storytelling, great dialogue, great everything.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What really scares me with this one is that apparently SCP-087 is inside a janitor closet within a possible school/university (students complaining about knocking.) So the outside of SCP-087 was fitted with padding to "disguise" it with the wall.

This means that wherever this campus is, students and workers for some kind of school spend most of their day walking and passing by the possible opening of this thing.

It's really scary how so many innocent and unprepared people spend each day so close to whatever is actually within SCP-087.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read this article so many times, that I've slipped right over that detail now. You got me on that one. I've written a 4th exploration log, and at least now I know my ending kind of works with that theory. woot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Special Containment Procedures state that the inside of the door is where the padding was added… Hm.

Assuming it's not some kind of mistake/not-well-thought-out-process, there might be a deliberate implication that somebody had to be sent in to confront 087-1 and do that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, it doesn't state that they put the padding on the inside. It says that the sound is coming from the inside, so they fitted the door itself with industrial padding. AKA, the exterior, has been fitted with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Zaeyde, if you are being serious, and you actually do have a fourth exploration log written, please, please release it. I know you expressed concern that speculation is one of the things that makes it compelling. The thing is, you can always release it as a separate story, not as a part of the main article. This would be similar to what people do when they post secrets on the "The Leak" thread, it would enrich the SCP for those who wish to know about it further without changing the article.

Also, frankly, this article has been scaring people for almost two decades and has been cemented as one of the best - if not the best - SCP on the site. I don't think you're going to detract from the article's quality by posting the fourth exploration log after all of this time. Moreover, you can say the old, "What's in your mind is scarier" bit, but I really do think you can come up with something better than I can. I think this SCP is pretty solid evidence of this

EDIT: Ok, I only just noticed this, but it's really bothering me. So, the Foundation starts to hear knocking, so they respond by lining the inside of the door with industrial padding. The INSIDE of the door. Correct me if I'm wrong, but the inside of the door is the part of the door that faces inside SCP-087, right? So that means the Foundation opened the door to SCP-087 in order to line in with padding, right?

See, I always pictured the knocking being Something Horrible on the other side of the door, but if the Foundation actually opened the door in order to line it, then that complicates things. What is making the knocking sound? It could be like the child pleading sounds, potentially a ruse created by something else, but those don't seem to occur until one passes the first flight of stairs. What is the knocking? Is it actually someone/something knocking, and if so, what happened during the time the Foundation opened the door to put the lining in?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read this article many MANY times over the last few years. I STILL can't get over Document 087-IV. I NEED TO READ IT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
though apparently no one is paying attention. :X

There are thousands of other pages on the site that other people could be reading aside from this one, and there are 18 more pages of discussion in this thread as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha I just noticed it myself… though apparently no one is paying attention. :X

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno how long it's been there or how long I've read right past it without noticing but: "what is to be a child" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a small grammatical question; "Subjects report and audio recordings confirm the distressed vocalizations from what is to be a child between the ages of █ and ██." (First line of Paragraph 2 in SCP-087's Description as of May 19th, 2017)

"…from what is to be a child…" ?
Should it not instead say something like, "What appears to be" or "What is estimated to be"?

I'd edit this myself, however it's quite possible this is just some more obscure syntax rule or else there is some context that eluded me. So, I decided to first point it out here, just to be safe.

(also this is my first time posting, so if I've done something weird/wrong, please inform me)

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Considering that you are showcasing a WIP article, review-soliciting would be the ultimate perception of the post. If it were posted on the site, the "end and understanding" aspect would be more prominent.

EDIT: As a wrap-up, potential reviewers, please go to the relevant forum thread to post your comments regarding the draft: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2240642/so-what-s-really-at-the-bottom-of-the-stairwell

Do not post comments regarding the draft here. This thread is for stuff that is currently on SCP-087.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: While I didn't post it to "solicit review", but solely to bring some kind of "end and understanding", it is completely understandable and do apologize for it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Synyster ZackyV, please note that discussion threads for articles are not the place to solicit reviews (however relevant they may be). They are mainly for discussing the article and its content, not an outlet to link one's drafts from.

Please solicit review via proper channels like the Drafts forum or chat or PM individuals whose opinions you desire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Erasing this embarrassing moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is by far the definition of timeless, at least for this site. While the description is particularly cliche, it's the documents that make this piece what it is. I'm quite disgruntled at how document 087-04 will forever be a mystery… but I guess it could be used as a personal canon of sorts. Also, that face will either be the most unnerving thing for many when they see it the first time or simply a scary ninja.

Ah yes, and the main picture is what really freaked me out, especially with the occasional appearance of that face, which for me didn't appear until I came back to re-read this article after turning on some Silent Hill 1 music… I don't particularly recommend doing that, especially at night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anglerfish use lights to lure their food in closer.
Other than noise that varies in volume, there is no indication of a crying child.
